item.title: Profs Anisur, Serajul join BOU as pro-VCs
item.description: Professor Anisur Rahman from the department of marketing and Professor Md Serajul Islam from the Institute of Education and Research at the University of Dhaka have joined as the new pro-vice-chancellors of Bangladesh Open University for a four-year term. ...

item.title: Afroza urges steps for correct onfo in aviation sector
item.description: Civil aviation and tourism minister Afroza Khanam on Saturday called upon all stakeholders to remain vigilant against misinformation and disinformation targeting the country’s aviation sector and government institutions....

item.title: Exhibition highlights women’s leadership in coastal climate resilience in Khulna
item.description: A day-long photography exhibition titled Voices from the Coast: A Journey of Climate Resilience was held in Khulna on Saturday, highlighting the leadership, resilience and adaptation efforts of women living in Bangladesh’s climate-vulnerable coastal regions....

item.title: Land minister for stronger family ties to curb violence against women, children
item.description: Land minister Mizanur Rahman Minu at a seminar in Rajshahi on Saturday said that building strong family bonds and raising children as responsible human beings was essential to preventing violence against women and children....

item.title: Badruddin Umar worked for society free of discrimination: speakers
item.description: Politicians, academics and professionals on Saturday at a seminar in Dhaka said that eminent author and politician Badruddin Umar worked lifelong to establish a discrimination free society in the country, where people would enjoy their rights....

item.title: Cat halts metro rail services for 20 minutes
item.description: Metro rail services in Dhaka city were closed for around 20 minutes after a cat fell onto the Shahbagh metro station’s high-voltage tracks at noon on Saturday....

item.title: Ukrainian strike kills one in southern Russia
item.description: A Ukrainian drone attack killed one person and wounded at least three others in southern Russia, a regional official said Saturday, with nearly 100 people fighting to extinguish a fire caused by the strike....

item.title: PM urges every citizen to plant tree to ensure clean environment
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman has urged every citizen of the country to plant at least one tree sapling to build a clean and pollution-free environment for the next generation....

item.title: Shafiqur urges PM to accept referendum verdict
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami amir and leader of the opposition in parliament Shafiqur Rahman on Saturday urged prime minister Tarique Rahman to accept the referendum verdict, warning of consequences if the people’s mandate was ignored....

item.title: RNPP to serve as a milestone in ensuring reliable energy: minister
item.description: Science and technology minister Fakir Mahbub Anam said the Rooppur Nuclear Power Plant (RNPP) will serve as a milestone in ensuring reliable, affordable and sustainable energy in the country....

item.title: Ansar to launch Japanese language training for female members
item.description: Bangladesh Ansar and Village Defence Party has taken a new initiative to enhance women’s empowerment, skill development and access to international job markets through Japanese language training for its female members....

item.title: Israel hits south Lebanon as Iran, US near deal
item.description: Lebanon reported Israeli strikes in the country’s south on Saturday, as the Israeli army issued evacuation warnings for the city of Nabatieh and more than 20 other locations ahead of raids....

item.title: US-Iran deal likely soon: Pakistan
item.description: The United States and Iran could finalise an agreement to end the Middle East war within as little as 24 hours, mediator Pakistan said Saturday, after both sides expressed optimism about the chances of concluding a deal....

item.title: Thousands rally against racism after Belfast unrest
item.description: Thousands of people on Saturday gathered for an anti-racism rally in the Northern Irish city of Belfast after unrest sparked by a horrific stabbing......

item.title: Ad-din to appeal licence revocation today
item.description: Ad-din Medical College Hospital at Moghbazar in the capital will appeal today for a review of a recent decision by the Directorate General of Health Services to cancel its licence over the deaths of six newborns on May 27....

item.title: N Korea protests Seoul-EU rebuke of Russia ties
item.description: North Korea denounced on Saturday a joint statement by South Korea and the European Union that condemned Pyongyang’s military ties with Russia throughout the Ukraine war......

item.title: External debt interest payments to double in FY29
item.description: External interest payments will continue to grow and will be almost double in the next three financial years, while the government is struggling to meet its overseas debt obligations due to both internal and external factors....

item.title: New sex abuse complaints against French star Bruel
item.description: Two women have filed new complaints of sexual abuse against French pop icon Patrick Bruel, prosecutors and their lawyer said Saturday, in the latest accusations against the singer under investigation......

item.title: 12 stranded along border as BGB-BSF flag meet fails
item.description: Members of the Border Guard Bangladesh foiled two more alleged push-in attempts by the Indian Border Security Force at border points in Lalmonirhat and Chapainawabganj between Friday night and early Saturday....

item.title: India FM calls Rubio over Gulf ship attacks
item.description: Indian foreign minister Subrahmanyam Jaishankar has called us secretary of state Marco Rubio in protest after American strikes on three largely Indian-crewed merchant vessels off Oman killed three Indians, upping the diplomatic ante......

item.title: Thousands gather in Bangkok to mourn late princess
item.description: The body of the Thai king’s eldest daughter was brought to the royal palace in Bangkok on Saturday, as thousands of black-clad mourners lined the procession route......

item.title: US businessman detained in Myanmar
item.description: An American consultant and author of a memoir about Myanmar’s 2021 coup is being detained in the country over a property dispute, a police source told AFP on Saturday......

item.title: Opposition figures injured in DR Congo rally clashes
item.description: Two opposition figures in the DR Congo were injured on Friday at a rally against government plans to change the constitution, broken up by police after clashes with pro-government supporters......

item.title: 123 India-registered Rohingyas pushed in by BSF still in BD
item.description: At least 123 Rohingyas who were registered with the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees in India are still being sheltered in Bangladesh’s overcrowded Rohingya camp......

item.title: 10 district hospitals to launch ICU services today
item.description: Intensive Care Unit services are set to be officially launched at 10 district-level hospitals across the country under the Emergency Response and Pandemic Preparedness project....

item.title: Plunderers’ punishment must precede Islami Bank rescue
item.description: THE Bangladesh Bank governor on June 12 said that the emergency liquidity support of Tk 100 billion that Islami Bank Bangladesh Plc had sought would be extended soon. The central bank governor, at a post-budget briefing, has also given an assurance that depositors of the bank can withdraw their deposits at any time, noting that the Bangladesh Bank has a number of tools......

item.title: Import dependency risks food security
item.description: WHEAT production is facing a steady, concerning decline. Over the past decade, wheat farming has fallen to its lowest level in years while production has remained largely stuck at about 10–12 lakh tonnes despite earlier gains in productivity. In the 2024–25 financial year, the government spent Tk 224,772 million on wheat import, with costs further inflated by volatile global......

item.title: Inclusive budget without inclusive foundations
item.description: THE national budget for the 2026–27 financial year is proposed as a journey towards a ‘democratic, humane and inclusive economy.’ It is an appealing narrative at a time when millions struggle with declining purchasing power, stagnant income, weak employment opportunities and growing uncertainty. Yet, budgets should be judged not by their aspirations but by their......

item.title: In need of prudent power policy
item.description: THE floor of the parliament is meant to be a place for democratic accountability, where the vulnerabilities of citizens are converted into state protection. But in a recent session, it transformed into a theatre of elite indifference. When Mohammad Akhtaruzzaman Bachchu, lawmaker for the Mymensingh 10 constituency, stood to tell a harrowing tale that 10-11......

item.title: Digital indigestion: mobile and algorithm
item.description: BANGLADESH has been in the mobile era for more than three decades although it has been in the thick of it for a decade and a half with the introduction of 3G network that began in 2012. What began basic calls and texts has now become a flood of video, algorithm and addiction reaching every home. A 14-year-old boy in a village now has access to the same world......

item.title: Kane hails England preparation
item.description: Harry Kane said England had enjoyed ‘great preparation’ ahead of their World Cup campaign as the squad touched down in Kansas City -- their base camp for the tournament....

item.title: Japan coach sorry for 'hurt' after ending Endo's dream
item.description: Japan coach Hajime Moriyasu on Saturday described Wataru Endo as ‘hurt’ and apologised after torpedoing the captain's World Cup dreams on the eve of the tournament....

item.title: Raya relaxed despite Spain goalkeeper debate
item.description: David Raya believes Spain ‘are in good hands’ no matter who starts the World Cup in goal as he battles with Unai Simon and Joan Garcia to be Luis de la Fuente's number one....

item.title: Vinicius earns Brazil draw with Morocco
item.description: Vinicius Junior scored a brilliant equaliser for Brazil in a 1-1 draw with Morocco on Saturday as the five-time World Cup winners made an uncertain start to the tournament in New Jersey....

item.title: Qatar earn first ever World Cup point with late goal
item.description: Qatar's Boualem Khoukhi stunned Switzerland with a late equaliser as the sides played out a 1-1 draw in their opening Group B match at the World Cup on Saturday....

item.title: Export competitiveness, inclusive growth key to economic drive
item.description: Policymakers on Saturday said Bangladesh must sharpen its export edge and extend economic opportunity to historically excluded groups, as the country navigates slowing global demand, rising trade barriers, and structural financing pressures that threaten to constrain growth......

item.title: Deposits in poor’s bank accounts slow
item.description: Deposits in accounts held by the extreme poor dipped in the January-March quarter, falling to Tk 277 crore, even as the total volume of these accounts continues to swell......

item.title: BSIA Silicon River Roadshow strengthens global connection
item.description: Bangladesh Semiconductor Industry Association wrapped up the Silicon Valley part of its Silicon River USA Roadshow 2026, achieving key engagements with global semiconductor leaders, tech firms, research institutions, investors, and Non-Resident Bangladeshi professionals to bolster Bangladesh’s goal of becoming a global innovation hub......

item.title: ICAB for coordinated action to meet FY27 revenue goal
item.description: The Institute of Chartered Accountants of Bangladesh on Saturday observed that the proposed national budget for 2026-27 financial year reflects the government’s commitment to economic stability, enhanced revenue mobilisation, employment generation, investment expansion and private sector development......

item.title: Govt proposes Tk 1,884cr for aviation, tourism
item.description: The government has proposed an allocation of Tk 1,884 crore for civil aviation and tourism ministry in the national budget for 2026-27 fiscal, rolling out visibly an ambitious roadmap to transform Bangladesh into a regional aviation and tourism hub......

item.title: Budget unveils ‘one ID-one digital wallet’ plan
item.description: The government has unveiled a plan to build an integrated digital state through the introduction of a ‘One Citizen-One ID-One Digital Wallet’ system, enabling citizens to access a wide range of public and private services through a single digital identity......

item.title: OpenAI, Anthropic warn of AI dangers
item.description: OpenAI and Anthropic, two AI companies heading for stock market debuts along with Elon Musk’s SpaceX, are sharpening their warnings about the risks of AI — even as they race to outpace each other with ever more powerful technology......

item.title: Messi plushies see roaring trade as China firms get World Cup boost
item.description: Toy charms shaped like a goat and decked out in Lionel Messi’s Argentina number 10 shirt pepper a factory tabletop in China, where sellers are betting on the country’s lucrative fan market for a big World Cup boost......

item.title: UAE property tycoon seeks to become data king
item.description: A UAE real estate magnate close to Donald Trump is pumping billions of dollars into data centres, hoping to cash in on the AI boom and become the global leader in the field......

item.title: Battle heats up over EU carbon market reform
item.description: With a major reform of Europe’s carbon market just a month away, EU states, industry and environmental groups are locked in battle over the bloc’s flagship climate tool......

item.title: Cuba opens more sectors to private business
item.description: Cuban president Miguel Diaz-Canel on Friday announced greater freedoms for small businesses across the country, as the communist government takes steps to liberalize the island’s economy in the face of a crippling US blockade......

item.title: Anthropic cuts access to AI models over US ‘national security’ order
item.description: Anthropic said Friday it has suspended access to two powerful AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, to comply with a US national security order......

item.title: Freelancing an engine towards trillion-dollar economy
item.description: Bangladesh is banking on its rapidly expanding freelancing and outsourcing sector to help achieve its ambitious goal of becoming a one-trillion-dollar economy by 2034, with the government rolling out sweeping tax incentives, startup support, digital infrastructure projects and skills development programmes to unlock the potential of millions of young people......

item.title: Limes emerge as fortune changer for Rangpur farmers
item.description: In recent years, lime cultivation has become a profitable venture, changing the fortunes of many farmers in the five districts of the Rangpur agricultural region......

item.title: Bank Asia approves 17pc dividends
item.description: Shareholders of Bank Asia PLC on June 11 approved 17 per cent dividends for the year 2025, comprising 8.5 per cent cash dividend and 8.5 per cent stock dividend, said a press release......

item.title: EBL approves 28pc dividends at 34th AGM
item.description: Eastern Bank PLC has approved 28 per cent dividends for the year ended December 31, 2025 comprising 25 per cent cash dividend and 3 per cent stock dividend, said a press release......

item.title: Karmasangsthan Bank opens 290th branch
item.description: Karmasangsthan Bank on Saturday opened its 290th branch at Dhobaura Upazila in Mymensingh, said a press release......

item.title: Ruchi Beautigram Season 7 concludes
item.description: In the seventh edition of ‘Ruchi Beautigram,’ content creators from different regions of the country presented Bangladesh through their own lenses and in their own voices......

item.title: PRAN Dairy, BAU partner for dairy sector dev
item.description: PRAN Dairy Limited and the Interdisciplinary Institute for Food Security of Bangladesh Agricultural University have recently signed an agreement to work together for the development of Bangladesh’s dairy industry, said a press release......

item.title: Walton launches Xanon X22 5G
item.description: Walton has introduced a new smartphone under its ‘Xanon’ series, the Xanon X22 5G, featuring advanced specifications and 5G network support, said a press release......

item.title: US-Iran deal to be signed Sunday, Hormuz to open afterwards: Trump
item.description: Iranian foreign ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei said earlier on Saturday that the date of the signing was yet to be determined, but ‘it will not be tomorrow’......

item.title: Road accident victims receive Tk 77 lakh in Jaipurhat
item.description: Bangladesh Road Transport Authority has distributed financial assistance of Tk 77 lakh among 21 families affected by a road accident in Jaipurhat, under its Road Transport Act Trust Fund....

item.title: Mango harvest peaks in Meherpur
item.description: The mango harvest is at its peak in Meherpur, with farmers, orchard owners and seasonal workers busy collecting one of the district’s most valuable seasonal crops amid expectations of higher production this year....

item.title: Constitutional recognition of nat’l minorities demanded
item.description: National minority leaders on Saturday once again raised their demand for constitutional recognition as indigenous peoples, while also pressed for a separate land commission for the plainsland national minority communities....

item.title: RMCH emergency unit shut down following assault on physician
item.description: Doctors at Rangpur Medical College Hospital suspended emergency medical services and staged a protest on Saturday after an on-duty physician was allegedly assaulted by relatives of a deceased patient....

item.title: 14 Chhatra League activists arrested over flash procession
item.description: The police on Saturday morning arrested 14 leaders and activists of banned Bangladesh Chhatra League allegedly for disrupting law and order by bringing out a flash procession in the capital’s Banani area....

item.title: Swechchhasebak Dal welcomes budget, GOP criticises weaknesses
item.description: Jatiyatabadi Swechchhasebak Dal on Saturday welcomed the proposed national budget for the financial year 2026-27 by bringing out a celebratory procession in the capital, while the Gono Odhikar Parishad......

item.title: Tech-skilled youth will build prosperous Bangladesh: ICT minister
item.description: Posts, telecommunications and information technology and science and technology minister Faqir Mahbub Anam on Saturday said that tech-skilled youth would build a prosperous Bangladesh....

item.title: ‘Red Feminist Bloc’ launched, justice for Kalpana Chakma demanded
item.description: A group of women rights activists on Saturday launched ‘Red Feminist Bloc’, aimed at addressing issues related to women’s rights, dignity, security, and empowerment....

item.title: Ibtedayi teachers to march towards secretariat today
item.description: A section of ibtedayi madrassah teachers on Saturday announced that they would hold a long march to the Bangladesh Secretariat in the capital Dhaka to meet the education minister today, demanding the nationalisation of all ibtedayi madrassahs....

item.title: Juba Dal leader among 2 arrested over Sonargaon woman ‘rape’ case
item.description: The police on Friday night arrested two men, including a local Juba Dal leader, on charges of allegedly raping a woman in the Sirab area of Jampur union under Sonargaon in Narayanganj....

item.title: Beyond the Surface depicts nature, human experiences
item.description: A five-day international group art exhibition titled Beyond the Surface, depicting nature and diverse human experiences, is under way at Gallery MAFA at Lalmatia in Dhaka....

item.title: Shahidul Zahir’s polyphonic voice challenged grand narratives: seminar
item.description: Writers, literary researchers and critics expressed that fiction writer Shahidul Zahir challenged the mainstream historical narratives through his polyphonic, memory-driven narratives....

item.title: Honouring luminaries during lifetime inspires social work: agri minister
item.description: Fisheries, livestock, and agriculture minister Mohammed Amin Ur Rashid on Saturday said that if the people who worked for the welfare of society were given proper respect and recognition during their lifetime, many more people......

item.title: ULAB, ACU explore new avenues for int’l collaboration
item.description: The University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh hosted a meeting with representatives of the Association of Commonwealth Universities on its campus in Dhaka on Thursday....
